business_ideas.idea: Guide runner for blind Paralympic athletes
Go Links for startups - simple human-readable URL shortcuts to docs
Nira - cloud document access audit and revocation platform
Coolers.co - AI color palette generator for design
Executive database with deep profiles and relationship mapping
Consulting course business - consulting.com model by Sam Ovens

predictions.text: Guide running will be his path to the Olympics despite not being elite alone
Paper cut companies that solve frequent small annoyances will become massive
Nira will become valuable for enterprise security compliance

disagreements: Sam argues Olympics represent optimizing life on arbitrary skills with huge sacrifice but minimal return. Shaan counters that Olympics represent culture, civil rights (Jesse Owens vs Hitler, Catherine Switzer), international unity, and refugee recognition. Shaan wins the debate with historical depth.
dynamics: Sam drives with provocative takes; Shaan responds with depth and historical examples. High energy back-and-forth. Sam is conversational, Shaan is structured. Both riff on frameworks and ideas. Sam is the contrarian; Shaan grounds ideas in business/product reality.
frameworks: Max Levchin's optimization trap: don't spend life optimizing for nothing, choose wisely what you optimize
Paper cut companies: solve frequent small annoyances that compound
12-star experience: define 1-star to 12-star product experience to expand what's possible
Moments between the moments: design experiences not just at peaks but in transitions
0.001% rule: price sensitivity threshold should scale with wealth (Nathan Barry wealth ladder)
learnings: Anything can be optimized to infinity, so pick what matters before you start
Small repeated annoyances (paper cuts) often make better business opportunities than big problems
Design details that users don't notice are what make products feel premium
Wealth changes how you should think about spending, but trauma from scarcity can override logic
Sports and art transcend culture and language in ways few other things do

banger_quotes.quote: Be careful because you can spend your whole life optimizing for nothing
All the magic is in the moments between the moments
The Olympics are an example of optimizing your life on the dumbest shit
Sports represents so much more than just optimizing the seconds

numbers_dropped: 0.001% rule: at $5M net worth = $500 price sensitivity threshold
Winter Olympics ratings at lowest ever
notable: Long form debate on whether Olympics represent cultural value or wasted optimization; pivots into multiple paper-cut business ideas with real examples (Nira, Coolers.co, Go Links) showing Shaan's investment thesis
